title: ～だらけ
titleEn: Indicates that something is full of or covered with a certain (usually negative) characteristic.
explanation: Indicates that something is full of or covered with a certain (usually negative) characteristic. (Ngu?n: Hanabira; b?n d?ch Vi?t c?n bi?n t?p.)
explanationEn: The ～だらけ grammar point is used to express that something is full of, covered with, or filled with a certain characteristic. It frequently has a negative or undesirable nuance.

title: ～っこない
titleEn: Expresses the impossibility or very low probability of something happening; 'no way', 'impossible'.
explanation: Expresses the impossibility or very low probability of something happening; 'no way', 'impossible'. (Ngu?n: Hanabira; b?n d?ch Vi?t c?n bi?n t?p.)
explanationEn: The ～っこない grammar point is used to express that something is impossible or extremely unlikely. It’s formed by attaching ～っこない to the negative stem of a verb. It can be translated as 'no way', 'no chance', or 'impossible.'

title: ～つつ
titleEn: Shows two actions happening simultaneously or a contrast; 'while', 'although'.
explanation: Shows two actions happening simultaneously or a contrast; 'while', 'although'. (Ngu?n: Hanabira; b?n d?ch Vi?t c?n bi?n t?p.)
explanationEn: The ～つつ grammar point is used to express two actions occurring simultaneously, or to show a contrast between an ongoing action and another factor. It can be translated as 'while' or 'although.' It is attached to a verb in its stem form.

title: ～つつある
titleEn: Expresses an ongoing process or gradual change.
explanation: Expresses an ongoing process or gradual change. (Ngu?n: Hanabira; b?n d?ch Vi?t c?n bi?n t?p.)
explanationEn: The ～つつある grammar point is used to describe something that is in the middle of a gradual change or development. It can be translated as '(something) is in the process of ~ing.' The most common usage is with the verb stem plus つつある.

title: ～っぱなし
titleEn: Express a continuous action or state; 'left as is', 'non-stop'.
structure: Verb-ます stem + っぱなし
structureEn: Verb-ます stem + っぱなし
explanation: Express a continuous action or state; 'left as is', 'non-stop'. (Ngu?n: Hanabira; b?n d?ch Vi?t c?n bi?n t?p.)
explanationEn: The ～っぱなし grammar point is used to express that something is left in a certain condition for an extended time or that an action continues without stopping. It attaches to the ます-stem of verbs.

title: ～っぽい
titleEn: Indicates that something has the appearance, characteristics, or behavior of something else.
structure: Noun + っぽい, Verb-casual + っぽい, い-Adjective + っぽい, な-Adjective + っぽい
structureEn: Noun + っぽい, Verb-casual + っぽい, い-Adjective + っぽい, な-Adjective + っぽい
explanation: Indicates that something has the appearance, characteristics, or behavior of something else. (Ngu?n: Hanabira; b?n d?ch Vi?t c?n bi?n t?p.)
explanationEn: The ～っぽい grammar point is used when something or someone appears to have characteristics, traits, or a vibe that resemble something else. It can mean 'looks like,' 'seems like,' or 'has the quality of.'

title: ～ていられない
titleEn: Expresses that one cannot afford to do something; 'can't keep on', 'unable to continue'.
structure: Verb-て form + いられない
structureEn: Verb-て form + いられない
explanation: Expresses that one cannot afford to do something; 'can't keep on', 'unable to continue'. (Ngu?n: Hanabira; b?n d?ch Vi?t c?n bi?n t?p.)
explanationEn: The ～ていられない grammar point is used to express a feeling of not being able to continue doing something or remaining in a certain state. It can be translated as 'can't keep on', 'unable to continue', or 'can't afford to' in English. The formation is the same for all verb types.

title: ～てかなわない
titleEn: Expresses that something cannot be endured, tolerated, or is unbearable.
structure: Adjective (〜く / 〜で) + て + かなわない / Verb phrase (reworded to an adjective/negative) + て + かなわない
structureEn: Adjective (〜く / 〜で) + て + かなわない / Verb phrase (reworded to an adjective/negative) + て + かなわない
explanation: Expresses that something cannot be endured, tolerated, or is unbearable. (Ngu?n: Hanabira; b?n d?ch Vi?t c?n bi?n t?p.)
explanationEn: The ～てかなわない grammar point is typically used with adjectives (or a rephrased verb) in 〜て form to show an unbearable situation or feeling. It means you 'can’t stand' or 'can’t endure' something. It often appears with expressions like 「暑くてかなわない」「うるさくてかなわない」 and so on.
